subject: UX & Research
title: The Hamburger Menu Debate
description: A fast breakdown of The Hamburger Menu Debate for builders.

hook.text: Three horizontal lines hide the entire navigation. Users call it the hamburger menu. Many never click it.

buildup.text: Hiding navigation behind a menu icon reduced discoverability. Users don't explore what they can't see.

discovery.text: Testing at NBC News showed that visible navigation got 20% more engagement than the hamburger menu version.

twist.text: Yet the hamburger persists because it saves screen space. It trades usability for visual cleanliness.

climax.text: The lesson: hidden navigation reduces clicks. Use it only when space is truly scarce and users know the paths.

punchline.text: Out of sight, out of mind. Show navigation when you can.

quiz.question: What did the NBC News test reveal about the hamburger menu?
options: Visible navigation got 20% more engagement than hidden menus
Users preferred the hamburger menu over tabs
